title: Bubbia parviflora
taxonomy: Bubbia parviflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to tropical Asia, specifically in countries such as China, India, and Southeast Asia. Bubbia parviflora belongs to the genus Bubbia, which is part of the broader order Sapindales. The plant is characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and compound leaves. As a member of the Sapindaceae family, Bubbia parviflora is closely related to other plants like the raintree and the lychee.
uses: Bubbia parviflora has been found to possess various medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
